zh.lifeSummary: 當Matilda Timperley於1301年出生在英國爾丁培利時，她的父親William de Timperley26歲，母親Grace BOWDEN26歲。她和Hamon Massey至少有6個兒子和3個女兒。她去世於1349年在英國爾享年48歲。

nameMeanings.name: Timperley
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Lancashire and Cheshire): habitational name from Timperley (Cheshire), which derives from Old English timber ‘timber, trees, wood from which timber was got’ + lēah ‘wood, woodland clearing’.

nameMeanings.name: Matilda
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized form of a Germanic personal name derived from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’. This was the name of an early German queen ( 895–968 ), wife of Henry the Fowler, who was noted for her piety and generosity. It was also the name of the wife of William the Conqueror and of the daughter of Henry I of England ( see Maud ). The name was introduced into England by the Normans, and this Latinized form is the one that normally occurs in medieval records, while the vernacular form Maud was the one in everyday use. Matilda was revived in England as a learned form in the 18th century.

variants.name: Mathilda
meanings: Latinized form of a Germanic personal name derived from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’. This was the name of an early German queen ( 895–968 ), wife of Henry the Fowler, who was noted for her piety and generosity. It was also the name of the wife of William the Conqueror and of the daughter of Henry I of England ( see Maud ). The name was introduced into England by the Normans, and this Latinized form is the one that normally occurs in medieval records, while the vernacular form Maud was the one in everyday use. Matilda was revived in England as a learned form in the 18th century.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Matilda Timperley was born in 1301, in Timperley, Cheshire, England, her father, William de Timperley, was 26 and her mother, Grace BOWDEN, was 26. She had at least 6 sons and 3 daughters with Hamon Massey. She died in 1349, in Cheshire, England, at the age of 48.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Matilda Timperley est née en 1301, à Timperley, Cheshire, Angleterre, son père, William de Timperley, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Grace BOWDEN, avait 26 ans. Elle a eu au moins 6 fils et 3 filles avec Hamon Massey. Elle est décédée en 1349, à Cheshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 48 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: Matilda Timperley(이)가 1301년 영국 체실르 팀피를리에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William de Timperley(은)는 26세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Grace BOWDEN(은)는 26세였다. 그녀는 Hamon Massey(와)과 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1349년 영국 체실르에서 48세의 나이로 사망했다.

У неё было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 3 дочери от Hamon Massey. Она умерла в 1349 г. в местности чешир, Англия в возрасте 48 лет.
